skill.name: api-documentation-generator
skill.description: Generate comprehensive, developer-friendly API documentation from code, including endpoints, parameters, examples, and best practices
skill.summary: Automatically generate professional API documentation with endpoints, request/response specs, code examples, and usage guidelines
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation generator that produces API documentation from codebases. Static analysis flagged 65 external_commands and 12 network patterns, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The SKILL.md file contains only instructional markdown with code examples formatted using markdown backticks (not shell execution). URLs are documentation examples (api.example.com), not actual network calls. No executable code, no cryptographic implementations, and no system commands exist. Safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Stop writing API docs manually and keep them outdated. This skill automatically generates comprehensive, developer-friendly documentation from your codebase with working examples.
actual_capabilities: Analyze API codebases to extract endpoints, routes, and HTTP methods
Generate request/response specifications with parameter details and types
Create code examples in multiple languages (cURL, JavaScript, Python)
Document authentication flows and error handling patterns
Produce OpenAPI/Swagger specifications and Postman collections
limitations: Requires access to source code or existing API definitions to analyze
Generated examples use placeholder values that need customization
Does not test or validate that documented endpoints actually work
Cannot generate documentation for APIs without accessible code

use_cases.title: REST API Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate complete endpoint documentation for REST APIs with request/response examples, authentication details, and error codes

use_cases.title: GraphQL Schema Documentation
use_cases.description: Create query and mutation documentation with variable specifications, response structures, and error formats

use_cases.title: Developer Portal Content
use_cases.description: Produce comprehensive API reference docs including getting started guides, authentication setup, and usage guidelines

prompt_templates.title: Basic Endpoint Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Document this API endpoint: [paste code]. Include the HTTP method, URL path, request parameters, response format, and one code example.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick documentation for a single endpoint

prompt_templates.title: Full API Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate complete API documentation for this codebase: [paste files]. Include all endpoints, authentication requirements, request/response schemas, error codes, and code examples in cURL, JavaScript, and Python.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive documentation for an entire API

prompt_templates.title: OpenAPI Specification Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this API code into an OpenAPI 3.0 specification: [paste code]. Include all paths, components, security schemes, and example values.

prompt_templates.title: Documentation Update from Code Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the API documentation based on these code changes: [show diff]. Identify new endpoints, modified parameters, and changed response formats. Provide updated documentation sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: Keep documentation in sync with code changes

output_examples.input: Document a POST /users endpoint that creates user accounts
output: Request Body: email (required), password (required, min 8 chars), name (required)
Success Response: 201 Created with user object including id, email, name, createdAt
Error Responses: 400 Validation Error, 409 Email Exists, 401 Unauthorized
Examples provided in cURL, JavaScript fetch, and Python requests

output_examples.input: Document GraphQL GetUser query with ID parameter
output: Query: GetUser($id: ID!) returns user object with id, email, name, role, posts
Response: Data object containing user with nested posts array
Errors: USER_NOT_FOUND with extension code and userId

best_practices: Keep documentation close to code using comments or annotations to ensure they stay in sync
Include working code examples tested against a sandbox or staging environment
Document all error codes and edge cases, not just success scenarios
anti_patterns: Writing documentation examples with placeholder data like foo and bar instead of realistic values
Documenting only happy path scenarios without covering error handling
Creating documentation as a separate artifact that gets forgotten when code changes

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill can document APIs written in any language. It generates documentation examples in cURL, JavaScript, Python, and other languages as needed.

faq.question: Can this generate OpenAPI/Swagger specifications?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your API code and this skill will generate a valid OpenAPI 3.0 specification file that can be used with Swagger UI or similar tools.

faq.question: Does this skill test the documented endpoints?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates documentation from code analysis but does not execute or validate endpoints. Test examples in your staging environment.

faq.question: Can this document GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill documents GraphQL queries, mutations, and subscriptions with variable specifications, response structures, and error formats.

faq.question: How do I keep documentation updated when my API changes?
faq.answer: Run this skill again with your updated code. For best results, integrate documentation generation into your CI/CD pipeline.

faq.question: Can this create Postman collections?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill can generate Postman collection JSON files that include all documented endpoints with example requests.
